headline: Mandatory HIV testing for migrants violates medical ethics
context: Malaysia's mandatory HIV testing requirement for all migrant workers was challenged in 2025 as violating medical ethics and human rights standards.

cards.big: 2.1 million migrant workers subjected to mandatory HIV testing annually
cards.sub: HIV-positive workers deported regardless of treatment availability or health status

cards.big: Immigration medical examination requires HIV, TB, and hepatitis testing
cards.sub: No informed consent process or pre-test counselling mandated by regulation

cards.big: UNAIDS and WHO oppose mandatory HIV testing as counterproductive since 2006
cards.sub: Forced testing drives epidemic underground and discourages voluntary testing

cards.big: Estimated 12,000 migrant workers deported for positive HIV results since 2015
cards.sub: Deportation to countries without treatment access effectively a death sentence

cards.big: Mandatory testing and deportation punishes illness instead of preventing transmission
Malaysian AIDS Council called for rights-based testing approach in 2024 report
